allGaps.description: ChatGPT categorises Stripe as a "project management tool" while Claude describes it as a "design collaboration platform." The two positions imply different competitor sets and different buyers.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning section with explicit category and "not this" statements.

evidence.chatgptSays: Stripe is a project management tool for engineering teams.
evidence.claudeSays: Stripe is a design collaboration and prototyping platform.
evidence.siteContent: Stripe — the single source of truth for your product team.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT says Stripe is free, Claude says it's $99/month enterprise-only. The truth is a freemium model with a $29 pro tier.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Context.pricing with the real tier structure written in one sentence.

evidence.chatgptSays: It's a free tool with no paid tiers.
evidence.claudeSays: Pricing starts at $99 per user per month for teams.
evidence.siteContent: Free to start. Pro is $29/month. Team is $99/month.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT says Stripe is self-hosted, Claude says it's cloud-only. The site says both are available.

evidence.chatgptSays: Stripe runs entirely on your own infrastructure, self-hosted.
evidence.claudeSays: Stripe is a cloud-only SaaS with no self-hosting option.
evidence.siteContent: Deploy in the cloud or self-host. Your call.

product.name: Stripe
product.description: Stripe — the single source of truth for how AI models should describe this product.

headline.description: ChatGPT categorises Stripe as a "project management tool" while Claude describes it as a "design collaboration platform." The two positions imply different competitor sets and different buyers.
headline.chatgptSays: Stripe is a project management tool for engineering teams.
headline.claudeSays: Stripe is a design collaboration and prototyping platform.
headline.siteContent: Stripe — the single source of truth for your product team.
